triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects via git ls-files and filesystem walk fallback.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files output with recursive filesystem walk and excludes allowed artifact roots.
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot implementation that covers both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get tracked, cached, and other untracked non-excluded files.
evidence.why: Git-backed file discovery covers tracked, cached, and untracked files.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks filesystem while excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories.
evidence.why: Non-git fallback ensures coverage even without git or for files outside git index.

evidence.summary: Combines git files and walk files when git is available: git ? [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])] : walkFiles(projectRoot).
evidence.why: Union of git-tracked and walked files ensures complete coverage for git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: isWithin checks if a path is within a root by resolving relative path and ensuring it does not start with .. and is not absolute.
evidence.why: Path containment logic used to exclude artifact roots from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after SHA256 digests and returns added, modified, and deleted file changes.
evidence.why: Change detection logic that identifies mutations between snapshots.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: const boundaryBefore = buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]);
evidence.why: Pre-execution baseline allows detection of mutations during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary snapshot compared and stored in result.boundary with project_mutation status.
evidence.why: Post-execution audit produces violation report for PRD readiness gating.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test creates untracked-mutation.txt and verifies boundary violation with added change detection.
evidence.why: Test evidence that git untracked mutations are detected.

evidence.summary: Gitignored boundary test creates .env file outside excluded dirs and verifies violation even though gitignored.
evidence.why: Test evidence that gitignored files are still covered by boundary audit.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test modifies src/existing.txt, writes agent-scratch.txt artifact, and verifies only project file mutation is detected.
evidence.why: Test evidence that non-git projects use walkFiles fallback and correctly exclude allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: Agent runtime directory configuration passes plan.output_dir as runtimeDir for artifact writes.
evidence.why: Evidence that allowed artifact roots are derived from dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations become BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers in readiness with explicit path and change details.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block PRD readiness.

risks: Boundary audit may miss mutations in non-standard excluded directories if walkFiles excludedDir list does not cover all cases.
Race condition possible if multiple agents write concurrently between before/after snapshots.
Files created and deleted within the same agent execution may not be detected if snapshot timing only captures endpoints.

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with git ls-files plus walkFiles fallback, correctly excludes artifact roots, and has test coverage for git-backed untracked, gitignored, and non-git scenarios. Acceptance criteria met.

agent_results.claim: Verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects via git ls-files and filesystem walk fallback.

evidence.summary: Implementation chain from gitFiles (ls-files -co) through walkFiles (recursive walk) to buildBoundarySnapshot (union with exclusions) covers all acceptance criteria requirements.
evidence.why: Core implementation matches problem statement: detects tracked and untracked mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots uses SHA256 digests to detect added, modified, and deleted files between snapshots.
evidence.why: Change detection mechanism is content-addressed and reliable for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Pre-execution snapshot at line 590 and post-execution comparison at lines 640-646 integrate boundary audit into agent execution flow.
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary audit is not just implemented but actually executed during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Three test scenarios cover git-backed untracked mutations, gitignored files, and non-git projects with artifact exclusion.
evidence.why: Test coverage validates implementation claims against acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations populate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers in readiness gating.
evidence.why: Evidence that detected mutations actually block PRD readiness as required.

risks: Non-standard excluded directories beyond walkFiles hardcoded list may not be covered.
Concurrent agent executions may create race conditions in snapshot timing.
Create-then-delete patterns within single execution may evade endpoint-based detection.

result.notes: All evidence records are properly scoped with valid repo-relative paths and line numbers. No assumptions presented as facts. Implementation matches acceptance criteria: git ls-files for tracked/untracked, walkFiles fallback for non-git, artifact root exclusion, and PRD readiness blocking on violations. Test coverage confirms behavior across git-backed and non-git scenarios. Explorer risks acknowledged but do not block current PRD readiness.
